config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat cara yang menghormati martabat dan kebebasan setiap orang. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, masyarakat justru berisiko menjadi tidak adil dan tidak aman bagi siapa pun.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in China we have seen how easily "the greater good" can be used to justify pressure, silence, or unfair treatment. At the same time, I do not think individual rights can be treated as absolute in every case, because public order, public health, and basic social stability do matter. The real issue is whether the society is protecting people fairly or just asking them to sacrifice while power stays unchecked. So for me, individual rights should set the boundary, and society should only override them in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam suara kecil, menekan minoritas, atau menutup penyalahgunaan wewenang, padahal justru di situlah negara harus paling hati-hati. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, karena masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi semua orang secara adil. Pengecualian bisa saja ada untuk hal yang benar-benar darurat, tetapi itu harus sempit, transparan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general rule, because in my view it is too easy for "society" to become a vague excuse for abuse. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize stability, public order, and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how individual rights can be neglected when authorities claim they know what is best for everyone. Society should matter, yet it should not sit above basic rights except in narrow, transparent, and genuinely necessary emergencies. If the collective always comes first, then the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice, and that is not a healthy or fair society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering jadi pintu masuk untuk membungkam kritik, menekan kelompok kecil, atau menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama, karena justru dari situ kepentingan publik bisa dijalankan secara adil, bukan sewenang-wenang. Pengecualian memang bisa ada saat darurat, tetapi harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji, supaya “masyarakat” tidak berubah jadi alasan untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in my view it is too easy for "society" to become a convenient excuse for suppressing people. Coming from China, I understand the appeal of collective responsibility and social stability, but I have also seen how quickly individual rights can be treated as secondary when officials claim they are acting for the common good. To me, a healthy society is not one that can demand the most sacrifice, but one that can protect basic rights even when it is inconvenient. Society should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly necessary, and accountable emergencies, not as a default rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
